seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: keep the min-cost degree-capped connectivity goal but (a) restrict the output to a per-node collar-capped connected spanning network (caps in {2,3,4}, degree-constrained => NP-hard) and (b) generalize the input from a Euclidean complete graph to an arbitrary weighted terrain graph given as an explicit buildable-link list with non-metric costs, turning the polynomial Euclidean MST into the NP-hard degree-constrained MST on a general graph.
title: Caldera Sensor Backbone: Degree-Capped Relay Mesh over Lava Terrain
strategies: chain baseline: build the guaranteed backbone links 1-2, 2-3, ..., (N-1)-N (always feasible, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)

degree-constrained Kruskal: sort buildable links by cost and add each shortest link that joins two components while both endpoints still have a free collar, repairing leftover components with any cap-respecting cross link, else fall back to the chain

Kruskal + cap-aware cycle-swap local search: from the degree-constrained tree, repeatedly insert a cheaper non-tree link and drop the most expensive link on the cycle it closes whenever the caps still hold, driving total cost well below the greedy tree
randomized/priority variants: bias link selection toward low-degree nodes or run multiple Kruskal orders and keep the cheapest feasible spanning mesh
